ocr.text: The physician should only physically examine the man if his semen analysis is irregular, classified as LOE C, according to the
medical-technical recommendations for general infertility for physicians, specifically for family physician (physical
examination). The gynecologist should not test ovarian reserve capacity to predict probability of pregnancy (with or without
treatment), classified as LOE A, as outlined in the recommendations for unexplained infertility. Additionally, the physician
should order a semen analysis from an accredited laboratory (ISO15189) or from a referral hospital, categorized as LOE D,
under the organizational recommendations for the organization of fertility care.
Medical-technical recommendations  Origin or guideline  Chapter of the MuG
The physician should only physically examine the man if his semen analysis is irregular. LOE C  General infertility for physicians  Family physician (physical examination)
The gynecologist should not test ovarian reserve capacity to predict probability of pregnancy (with or without treatment). LOE A  Unexplained infertility  Gynecologist (assessments)
Organizational recommendations
The physician should order a semen analysis from an accredited laboratory (ISO15189) or from a referral hospital. LOE D  MuG  Organization of fertility care

In accordance with the Dutch IVF planning decree, every licensed IVF center and their corresponding transport and satellite centers must provide annual reports on treatment outcomes for uniform national IVF registration. LOE D  MuG  Organization of fertility care (registration)
Patient-centered recommendations
Both partners of the couple should be involved in the assessment and management of infertility because it is a joint problem. LOE C  MuG  Physician, gynecologist, and urologist (basic principles)
The gynecologist should offer couples with fertility problems psychological support throughout all phases of fertility care. LOE D  MuG  Gynecologist (information provision)
Patient recommendations
Patients want their gynecologist to inform them about the different phases of treatment and their expected time spans. LOE P  MuG  Gynecologist (information provision)
Patients want their physician to make a referral immediately after they have been trying to conceive for 1 year. LOE P  MuG  physician (referral)
In accordance with the Dutch IVF planning decree, every licensed IVF center and their corresponding transport and satellite
centers must provide annual reports on treatment outcomes for uniform national IVF registration, also classified as LOE D.
Patient-centered recommendations emphasize that both partners of the couple should be involved in the assessment and
management of infertility because it is a joint problem, noted as LOE C, and the gynecologist should offer couples with fertility
problems psychological support throughout all phases of fertility care, categorized as LOE D. Regarding patient
recommendations, patients want their gynecologist to inform them about the different phases of treatment and their expected
time spans, indicated as LOE P, and they want their physician to make a referral immediately after they have been trying to
conceive for 1 year, also classified as LOE P. The gynecologist's role in information provision is underscored in each of these
recommendations, demonstrating the importance of comprehensive care in fertility management.
